behavior_summary: U8P_BRIBOOK3::func0080 launches a Book::read process to present a fixed block of book/lore text to the player and immediately suspends the current script.

The function passes a pointer to a long string (the book contents) and the current object reference (this) to Book::read, captures the returned process id into a local named temp, and then yields/suspends.
Invocation is not shown in the snippet but is likely triggered by player interaction with the book object (e.g., a 'read' or use action).
notable_control_flow: Call to external Book::read(text_ptr, this) — returns a process id which is stored in local temp.
Immediate suspend after spawning the read process: the script yields control while the separate Book::read process runs.
No branching, menu, or flag logic present in the provided code — behavior is linear and deterministic.
open_questions: What exact in-game event dispatch calls U8P_BRIBOOK3::func0080 (player 'read' action, use, or engine-driven trigger)?
Does Book::read ever write or consult global flags tied to progress or unlocking subsequent interactions (not visible in this snippet)?
Is the long text literal the canonical/complete content or a pointer to a localized resource (text was embedded as a string in the disassembly but could map to localization keys)?

main_function.name: U8P_BRIBOOK3::func0080
main_function.purpose: Spawn a Book::read process to show a fixed book/lore text to the player, track the spawned process id, and suspend the current script while the read process executes.
entry_conditions: Appears to be invoked when the player interacts with or attempts to read the book object (inferred).
No explicit conditional checks or flags are present in the function body.

function_map.name: U8P_BRIBOOK3::func0080
function_map.role: Start a book-reading UI/process and suspend the current script while that reading runs.

function_map.name: Book::read
function_map.role: External UI/process function that displays provided text as a book reading and returns a process id/handle.

external_calls.purpose: Display the provided long book/lore text to the player in a separate read-process; returns a process id which is stored locally so the script can track the spawned process.
